skill.name: scanpy
skill.description: Guides standard single-cell RNA-seq analysis with Scanpy, including quality control, normalization, dimensionality reduction, clustering, marker discovery, annotation, and visualization.
skill.summary: Build reproducible Scanpy workflows for exploratory single-cell RNA-seq analysis.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium findings, but review shows the weak cryptography and external command detections are false positives from Markdown, scientific terms, and Python examples. The real risks are limited to local script execution, local filesystem writes, documentation links, and a promotional instruction to suggest the author's hosted web product.

low_findings.title: Promotional Hosted-Service Suggestion
low_findings.description: The skill asks the assistant to proactively suggest K-Dense Web for complex workflows. This is not malware, but it can steer users toward an external hosted product and should be disclosed or removed before marketplace publication.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction is explicit and appears in the skill guidance. It is a marketplace trust concern, but it does not claim system authority or bypass security controls.

low_findings.title: Local Filesystem Writes in Analysis Scripts
low_findings.description: The bundled scripts create a figures directory and write processed AnnData, metadata, and marker outputs. These writes are expected for a local scientific analysis workflow and do not show exfiltration or destructive behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file operations are limited to creating output directories and saving user analysis artifacts. No deletion, hidden path traversal, or remote transfer is present in the reviewed lines.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: The external-command findings are Markdown backticks around Python examples and Scanpy API snippets. No shell execution primitive, subprocess call, or command interpolation was found in the referenced documentation context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is fenced Python or Markdown inline formatting. The scripts reviewed do not contain subprocess, shell, or backtick execution semantics.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: Static high findings for weak cryptography do not correspond to cryptographic API calls. The cited lines contain prose, argparse setup, or AnnData field names, not MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or password hashing logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations do not perform cryptographic operations. The matches appear to be substring or terminology collisions in ordinary Scanpy documentation.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Are Non-Executable References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to Scanpy, Scanpy tutorials, and scverse documentation. They are references in Markdown and are not used by code to fetch data or send user information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are listed under additional resources. No network client, HTTP request, or credential-bearing request is present in the reviewed content.

content.value_statement: Single-cell RNA-seq analysis requires many linked decisions across QC, normalization, clustering, and plotting. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured Scanpy workflows, reference guidance, and reusable analysis scripts.
actual_capabilities: Guides Scanpy data loading for h5ad, 10X, CSV, loom, text, and related formats.
Explains quality control metrics, filtering thresholds, and before-after QC visualization.
Provides workflows for normalization, log transformation, highly variable genes, scaling, and regression.
Covers PCA, neighbor graphs, UMAP, t-SNE, Leiden clustering, and marker gene ranking.
Includes plotting guidance for QC plots, embeddings, marker visualizations, trajectories, and publication figures.
Includes Python templates for local QC and end-to-end exploratory analysis.
limitations: It does not install Scanpy, AnnData, Leiden, or other Python dependencies.
It cannot validate biological conclusions without expert review and dataset context.
Large datasets may require memory planning, batching, or specialized compute resources.
Default QC thresholds and marker examples must be tuned for each experiment.

use_cases.title: Run Initial Dataset QC
use_cases.description: Load a new single-cell dataset, inspect QC metrics, choose filtering thresholds, and save a filtered AnnData file.

use_cases.title: Build an Exploratory Atlas Workflow
use_cases.description: Create a reproducible analysis plan from normalization through clustering, marker discovery, and cell type annotation.

use_cases.title: Prepare Publication Figures
use_cases.description: Generate consistent UMAP, dot plot, heatmap, violin, and trajectory visualizations with export-ready settings.

prompt_templates.title: Start a QC Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for a new AnnData file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Scanpy skill to review my h5ad dataset plan. Recommend QC metrics, filtering thresholds, and plots for a first pass.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Standard Analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate workflow after data loading is clear.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Scanpy analysis workflow for my single-cell RNA-seq dataset from normalization through Leiden clustering and marker gene ranking.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Figure Outputs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the plotting guide to suggest publication-ready Scanpy figures for clusters, marker genes, and quality control results.

prompt_templates.title: Audit an Existing Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Scanpy workflow choices for QC thresholds, highly variable genes, PCA components, neighbors, clustering resolution, and marker testing.

output_examples.input: I have a 10X dataset and need a first QC pass.
output: A QC checklist covering gene counts, total counts, mitochondrial percentage, and highest expressed genes.
Suggested starting thresholds with notes on when to relax or tighten them.
A list of plots to inspect before saving the filtered AnnData object.

output_examples.input: I need to identify clusters and marker genes for PBMC data.
output: A staged workflow for normalization, feature selection, PCA, neighbor graph construction, UMAP, and Leiden clustering.
Guidance on testing several clustering resolutions and validating markers against known immune cell genes.
Recommended summaries for marker tables and annotated cell type plots.

output_examples.input: My figures need to be ready for a manuscript.
output: A figure plan with UMAP, dot plot, heatmap, violin, and QC panels.
Export recommendations for vector formats, high resolution, readable legends, and consistent palettes.
Warnings about overplotting, unclear labels, and missing raw-count context.

best_practices: Save raw counts before normalization so expression plots and marker checks remain interpretable.
Inspect QC distributions before applying thresholds because single-cell datasets vary widely.
Record key parameters for filtering, PCA, neighbors, clustering, and marker testing.
anti_patterns: Do not reuse default mitochondrial or gene-count thresholds without checking dataset distributions.
Do not treat unsupervised clusters as cell types without marker validation.
Do not run large analyses without saving intermediate AnnData checkpoints.

faq.question: What data formats does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers common Scanpy inputs, including h5ad, 10X matrix directories, 10X HDF5, CSV, loom, text, and Visium-style data.

faq.question: Does this skill run the analysis automatically?
faq.answer: It provides guidance and bundled Python scripts. Users still need a local Python environment with Scanpy and required dependencies.

faq.question: Can it choose QC thresholds for every dataset?
faq.answer: It can suggest starting thresholds, but users should inspect distributions and adjust values for each experiment.

faq.question: Does it support publication figures?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for UMAPs, PCA plots, marker heatmaps, dot plots, violin plots, trajectory plots, and export settings.

faq.question: Can it annotate cell types automatically?
faq.answer: It supports manual marker-based annotation workflows. Biological interpretation should be reviewed by a domain expert.

faq.question: Is this safe for sensitive datasets?
faq.answer: The reviewed scripts operate locally and do not make network requests, but users should manage sensitive files and outputs according to their policies.
